actionable_feedback: Include a machine-readable export of the key formula cells (for example: CSV or plain-text listing of formula expressions including sheet and cell addresses for Inputs, Scoring, Sensitivity, and Validation). This lets graders/verifiers confirm formulas without relying on openpyxl.

Ensure the chemistry_note.md contains the full Recommendation / intervention-cost section (not truncated). The recommendation must include: (1) a table of proposed interventions with unit costs and totals, (2) explicit mapping from each intervention line to affected record IDs, and (3) a short calculation showing the intervention fits within decision_budget_usd=182500 and why it is the smallest defensible option.
Attach one or two worked sensitivity recalculation examples (explicit intermediate arithmetic) for a representative record and shifted scenario so automated re-computation is straightforward.

Produce an explicit 'formulas_export.txt' that lists the exact formulas used (sheet!cell -> formula) and a short validation note confirming the XLSX contains no macros. If possible, also provide a minimal CSV of computed baseline metrics for each record.
Canonicalize grader/verifier metadata before publishing: remove duplicated/contradictory passed_criteria/failed_criteria fields so downstream verifiers can read a single consistent block.

If the environment may again lack openpyxl, also include a self-contained plain-CSV or JSON representation of computed outputs (normalized metrics, weighted_score, rank, top_quartile, needs_review, actionability) so automated checks can re-run arithmetic independently.
feedback_summary: The submission meets the artifact contract and documents methods, per-record scoring, and sensitivity protocol; however automated verification was limited by an environment inability to open the XLSX and by a truncated chemistry_note that omitted the Recommendation / intervention-cost table. The grader and verifier also show a small metadata inconsistency (duplicated/contradictory passed/failed fields). Because cost/accounting evidence is not visible, ri_4 received partial credit.
